operating_state: Second quarter 2022 earnings reached $17.9 billion driven by 71% higher crude oil realizations and 186% higher natural gas realizations compared to prior year. Upstream production increased to 3.7 million oil-equivalent barrels daily with Permian and Guyana growth offsetting lower entitlements and divestments. Energy Products margins improved substantially due to elevated industry refining margins and favorable derivative effects, while Chemical Products earnings declined as higher feed and energy costs outweighed price increases.
cost_structure: Energy Products benefited from substantial margin improvement driven by industry refining capacity constraints and supply chain disruptions. Chemical Products faced margin compression as feed and energy cost increases exceeded pricing gains. Corporate expenses decreased reflecting favorable one-time tax impacts.
material_events: Corporation announced exit from Russia Sakhalin-1 project resulting in $3.4 billion after-tax impairment charges in first quarter. Effective April 1, 2022, streamlined business structure by combining Chemical and Downstream operations into single Product Solutions business. Distributed $7.5 billion in dividends and repurchased $6.1 billion of common stock during first half of 2022.
macro_exposures: Russia-Ukraine conflict and subsequent sanctions created supply tightness driving commodity prices to multi-year highs while exacerbating existing supply chain constraints. Kazakhstan oil exports face potential disruption through CPC pipeline traversing Russia, which could impact earnings from Corporation's interests. High commodity prices began tempering demand for some products by quarter end.
forward_outlook: Corporation plans capital expenditures of $21-24 billion for 2022, up 37% from prior year first half spending. Capital allocation priorities remain investing in advantaged projects, strengthening balance sheet, and paying reliable dividend. Management expects commodity and product price volatility to continue given global economic uncertainty and geopolitical events affecting supply and demand.
uncertainty_profile: Ongoing IRS disputes for tax years 2006-2011 with appeals proceeding in Fifth Circuit Court, though unfavorable resolution would not materially impact operations. Potential expansion of Russia sanctions could disrupt Kazakhstan oil transportation through CPC pipeline affecting cash flows. Efforts to transition Sakhalin-1 operatorship expected to result in minimal future hydrocarbon sales and cash flows.
